en: Thanks, Jimbo. Why don't you go ahead and give me and Mike here a little privacy?
What do you want, Gallo? That's a stupid question after what you pulled at my parole hearing.
You didn't deserve to get out. Neither did you. Now you're not going to.
What, you're going to kill me in the cell? There are guards out there.
Just doesn't sink in for you, does it? Those guards out there work for me.
See, Mike, I wasn't going to let what happened last time happen this time.
No one's going to come run helping you at the last second because they're all locked up tight in their bunks.
An irony of ironies, because of some prisoner's rights lawsuit way back when.
There are no cameras in cells. Wait, wait, wait, wait, wait, wait. No, time for waiting is done.
No, no, no, no, no, no, I meant, don't you want to hear about the camera that's in here?
Smile, you son of a bitch. No!
You did not keep yourself in here just to do this. Oh, yeah, Frank, I did.
And now all those guards that aren't in your pocket, they're watching this right now.
And it's 60 feet from that guard station to this cell.
So if I calculated correctly, they've got Jimbo in cuffs,
and they're going to be opening that door right now.
Drop that knife and step back, Gowell. You piece of shit.
Doesn't change anything. Yeah, yeah, yeah, we'll see about that.
Oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh,
Spending his life living through regret.
She never realized what's important.
Did it work? It worked.
We did it. We did.
Come on, let's get out of here. Oh, one more thing.
Someone couldn't wait for you to get home.
Would you bare, would you bare my love?
Would you bare, would you bare my love?
Would you bare, would you bare my love?
Would you bare, would you bare my love?
